This is consistent with expected post-transaction maintenance in Arch Linux packages.

Such hooks are a standard packaging practice for applications requiring index regeneration after file changes. The behavior enhances usability without introducing malicious capabilities. The actions are transparent, narrowly scoped, and use only trusted local scripts, meeting expected security and hygiene standards for AUR packages.
